posts.name: vxunderground
posts.text: R to @vxunderground: It's very surreal. It doesn't feel like typical depression. I've been depressed. This is a different feeling. My logical state is normal, I can identify everything is fine. However, my emotional state is shot to shit. It's weird because like, one side of brain is normal, one side of brain is depressed, but it doesn't match, it is very strange and very confusing. Because of this mismatch I can feel sad, then normal, then sad, then normal. It oscillates throughout the day, but it's not like mania or Bipolar disorder. It's like... Literally a random wave of depression that subsides seemingly randomly. It's really weird. Overall I give this experience a 3/10. It's not terrible, but it is far from good.
posts.text_zh: R 致@vxunderground：這非常超現實。感覺不像典型的憂鬱症。我一直很鬱悶。這是一種不同的感覺。我的邏輯狀態正常，我可以確定一切都很好。然而，我的情緒狀態卻一團糟。這很奇怪，因為就像，大腦的一側是正常的，大腦的一側是抑鬱的，但它不匹配，這是非常奇怪和非常令人困惑的。由於這種不匹配，我會感到悲傷，然後正常，然後悲傷，然後正常。它全天都在波動，但它不像躁狂症或雙相情感障礙。這就像……從字面上看，一波隨機的憂鬱症似乎隨機消退。真的很奇怪。總的來說，我給這次體驗打3/10。這並不可怕，但也遠非好。

posts.text: Lots of people asked me to be their Benzodiazepine test dummy and kind of document my taper and stuff. Many people seemed nervous trying to quit. Here is the current state. Unfortunately it is not good. > be me > on Benzodiazepines > 14 years > not good > decide to stop > cut by 25% > not too bad > people say be careful > can be hard > no big deal > cut another 25% > hehe 50% reduction > shrimple > day 5 arrives of 50% cut > feel weird > whatever > day 6 feel weirder > day 8 arrives > deep unfathomable depression > feel empty, unable to even malware Holy cannoli, Chat. This will pass, but you have GOT to be prepared when doing big tapers with Klonopin or Benzodiazepines. I know tons of people told me, but the first aggressive 25% cut wasn't bad. My brains GABA thingies are screaming at me and it feels like my soul is being crushed. It is a deep hollow emptiness and wave after wave of anxiety. Here is a silly picture depicting how I currently feel inside. Klonopin is not cool and it is not badass. It fucks you up
posts.text_zh: 很多人要求我成為他們的苯二氮平類藥物測試假人，並記錄我的減量和其他東西。許多人在試圖戒菸時顯得很緊張。這是當前狀態。不幸的是，這並不好。 > 做我自己 > 服用苯二氮卓类药物 > 14 年 > 不好 > 决定停止 > 减少 25% > 还不错 > 人们说要小心 > 可能很难 > 没什么大不了的 > 再减少 25% > 呵呵减少 50% > 虾 > 第 5 天到达 50% 减少 > 感觉很奇怪 > 无论如何 > 第 6 天感觉更奇怪 > 第 8 天到达 > 深不可测的抑郁 >感到空虛，甚至無法惡意軟體 聖甜甜餡煎餅卷，聊天。這會過去，但在使用氯諾平或苯二氮平類藥物進行高劑量減量時，您必須做好準備。我知道很多人告訴我，但第一次大幅削減 25% 還不錯。我的大腦中的 GABA 東西正在向我尖叫，感覺我的靈魂正在被壓碎。那是一種深深的空虛和一波又一波的焦慮。這是一張愚蠢的圖片，描繪了我目前的內心感受。 Klonopin 並不酷，也不壞。真讓你操蛋

posts.text: 🇧🇷 Brazilian Payment Provider Allegedly Compromised A threat actor claims to have compromised PagBank, one of Brazil's largest payment service providers, and is threatening to release allegedly stolen data on dark web marketplaces. According to the post, the actor claims: * Unauthorized access to the provider's core infrastructure * More than 250,000 active merchants allegedly affected * Over 1 billion historical transaction records * Alleged exposure of: * Transaction metadata * Merchant settlement information * POS terminal identifiers * The group claims the data has been "secured and verified" and is preparing it for distribution. The post also includes unverified claims describing the alleged intrusion methodology, including phishing, lateral movement, reverse engineering of internal APIs, and data exfiltration. Analyst Note: These claims remain unverified. Threat actors frequently exaggerate the scope and sophistication of alleged compromises to increase pressure on victims or attract buyers. No independent evidence currently confirms the claimed impact or authenticity of the dataset. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Brazil

posts.text: In an era where companies need to become more efficient and diverse they’ve basically picked the least efficient way to do it, with the biggest risks and highest costs - because everybody else is doing it. I know somebody at one of the big 4 who has written something in Claude that prompts Claude each twenty minutes for a question, then feeds Claude’s question back into Claude to use their tokens - because token usage is factored into employee evaluations. What are we even doing.
posts.text_zh: 在一個公司需要變得更有效率和多樣化的時代，他們基本上選擇了效率最低的方式來實現這一目標，同時風險最大，成本最高——因為其他人都在這樣做。我認識四大會計師事務所之一的某人，他在 Claude 中寫了一些東西，每二十分鐘提示 Claude 一個問題，然後將 Claude 的問題反饋給 Claude 以使用他們的代幣 - 因為代幣的使用情況會被納入員工評估中。我們到底在做什麼。

posts.name: GossiTheDog
posts.text: This isn’t, btw, a Kevin Doesn’t Use AI rant. I use GenAI extensively for various testing things. I had access to Mythos before almost everybody. I pay for Copilot Pro+. I pay for Gemini. I AI generate terrible songs. I vibecode security scanners. My personal spend exceeds £500 this month to date. Do I think you should make every employee depend on these third party GenAI tools for their job? No. Its ridiculous. You’re also *locked in* to costs you can’t afford which *will rise*. It’s a cliff.
posts.text_zh: 順便說一句，這並不是凱文不使用人工智慧的咆哮。我广泛使用 GenAI 进行各种测试。我比几乎所有人都接触过神话。我支付了 Copilot Pro+ 的费用。我為雙子座付錢。我人工智能生成了糟糕的歌曲。我使用 vivicode 安全扫描仪。到目前為止，我的個人支出本月已超過 500 英鎊。我認為您是否應該讓每位員工都依賴這些第三方 GenAI 工具來完成工作？不，這很荒謬。您還「鎖定」了您無法承受的成本，而這些成本「將會上升」。這是一個懸崖。

posts.name: GossiTheDog
posts.text: I’m really serious about this one btw. Companies have no measurable way of knowing what employees are doing with GenAI. They’re giving Claude Code out like it’s candy and just presuming everybody is an IT power user. They aren’t. They’re converting PDFs and vibe coding garden planning tools. Copilot M365 has a fake dashboard showing how productive people are.. it has no actual data. It just shows people use it. It’s CIO porn for the CEO. Orgs are pissing money up a wall worldwide.

posts.text: 🤖 EcoGPT Allegedly Exposed Shared AI Conversations A threat actor claims to have breached EcoGPT, an AI chatbot platform, alleging an insecure Supabase configuration exposed users' shared conversations. According to the post: * The actor claims the `shared_conversations` table was accessible without authentication. * Allegedly obtained data includes: * Conversation ID * Original conversation ID * User ID * Conversation title * Full chat messages * Creation timestamp * The leaked archive reportedly contains both JSON and CSV exports of shared conversations. * The actor claims they could not locate a separate users table and states the exposed data is limited to shared conversations. The post further alleges the issue stemmed from improper access controls rather than a compromise of Supabase itself. Analyst Note: These claims are currently unverified. If accurate, the incident would highlight the importance of enforcing Row Level Security (RLS), properly restricting public database endpoints, and reviewing access policies for AI applications handling user-generated conversations. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #AI

posts.name: vxunderground
posts.text: Nah, I have to share some of my personal experiences for a second. I saw a few newer people in information security share some code, or ideas they had, on social media. They retracted their posts, or code, because of criticism they received. This wasn't one singular person, to my surprise I saw like, five noobies sharing stuff. Very cool. Let me tell you something, stinky new people: yes, people online are mean. They are very mean. I have been BOILED ALIVE by stinky nerds online. I have been called every synonym for idiot you can imagine over the past ... uhhh, 21 years of doing malware related stuff. And because this is the internet, and we're connected across the globe, I've had the pleasure of being called an idiot (or something similar) in English, Spanish, French, Russian, Mandarin, Hindi, Arabic, Portuguese, and more. While these people are not nice, I very sincerely recommend filtering out the negative verbiage and focusing on the underlying message (if applicable). It is a skill that you'll need to adapt and grow in this field. In the "hacker" sphere, intellectuality is like, the thingie people value the most (or at least claim to). Hence, if you share something it will be heavily critiqued. When you share something, whether you explicitly say it or not, you're inviting your peers for feedback. You WILL receive feedback, good or bad, and you will have to learn to handle it, process it, or learn from it. Someone say your code is trash? Good. That is an opportunity to improve. Someone criticize you for leaning heavily on AI? Good. Use AI as a tool to learn and stop using it as much. Someone misunderstood what you said and called you a bad name? Good. Use that as an opportunity to practice improving your phrasing and writing. Someone say your idea is repetitive and has been done before? Good, you discovered an idea for yourself, learned about an existing idea, and now you know how to find more ideas.
posts.text_zh: 不，我必須分享我的一些個人經歷。我看到一些資訊安全領域的新人在社群媒體上分享了一些程式碼或他們的想法。由於收到批評，他們撤回了自己的貼文或代碼。這不是一個人，令我驚訝的是，我看到了五個菜鳥在分享東西。非常酷。讓我告訴你們一件事，臭新人：是的，網路上的人都很卑鄙。他們非常刻薄。我已經被網路上的臭書呆子活活煮死了。在過去，我被稱為白痴的所有同義詞……呃，21 年以來我一直在做與惡意軟體相關的事情。因為這是互聯網，而且我們在全球範圍內相互聯繫，所以我很高興被用英語、西班牙語、法語、俄語、普通話、印地語、阿拉伯語、葡萄牙語等稱為白痴（或類似的東西）。雖然這些人並不友善，但我非常真誠地建議過濾掉負面言論並專注於潛在的訊息（如果適用）。這是您需要在該領域適應和發展的技能。在「駭客」領域，智力就像人們最重視（或至少聲稱）的東西。因此，如果你分享一些東西，就會受到嚴厲的批評。當你分享某件事時，無論你是否明確地說出來，你都在邀請你的同事提供回饋。你會收到回饋，無論好壞，你都必須學會處理它、處理它或從中學習。有人說你的程式碼是垃圾？好的。這是一個改進的機會。有人批評你過度依賴人工智慧？好的。使用人工智慧作為工具來學習並停止使用它。有人誤解你的話說你壞話嗎？好的。以此為契機，練習提升措詞和寫作水準。有人說你的想法重複，以前已經有人做了？很好，您自己發現了一個想法，了解了一個現有的想法，現在您知道如何找到更多想法。

posts.text: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Forum Database Allegedly Leaked A threat actor has released what they claim is a free database belonging to http://eom.com.ua, advertising approximately 17,000 user records that include email addresses and password hashes. posts.text: 🚀 Japan Successfully Tests Reusable Rocket Prototype Japan’s space agency JAXA has completed the first flight test of its RV-X experimental reusable rocket at the Noshiro Rocket Testing Center. * Reached an altitude of approximately 11 meters. * Traveled around 16 meters horizontally. * Landed vertically as planned. * The entire flight lasted less than one minute. * Future tests are expected to target altitudes of roughly 100 meters. The test came one day after China successfully recovered the booster of its Long March 10B rocket using a sea-based net system. Analyst Note: Reusable launch technology is rapidly becoming a strategic capability. Japan’s test remains small-scale compared with operational orbital boosters, but it represents an important step toward reducing launch costs and strengthening domestic space access. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Japan
posts.text_zh: 🚀 日本成功測試可重複使用火箭原型 日本太空總署 JAXA 已在能代火箭測試中心完成了 RV-X 實驗性可重複使用火箭的首次飛行測試。 * 達到約11公尺的高度。 * 水平移動約 16 公尺。 * 按計畫垂直降落。 * 整個飛行持續了不到一分鐘。 * 未來的測試預計將瞄準大約 100 公尺的海拔高度。這次測試是在中國利用海基網路系統成功回收長徵十號乙火箭助推器的一天後進行的。分析師註：可重複使用的發射技術正迅速成為一種戰略能力。與運行中的軌道助推器相比，日本的測試規模仍然較小，但它代表著降低發射成本和加強國內太空准入的重要一步。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Japan

posts.text: 🇦🇪 Threat Actor Claims to Sell 400K DAMAC Properties Records A threat actor is advertising the sale of what they claim is a freshly obtained database belonging to DAMAC Properties, one of the UAE's largest real estate developers. posts.text: 🚨 Threat Actor Claims to Sell Massive "Truecaller Bot" Database A threat actor is advertising what they claim is the complete database behind a Telegram "Truecaller bot" that aggregates phone number intelligence from multiple sources, including Truecaller and CallApp. According to the listing: * Claimed dataset size: Nearly 7 billion unique records * Format: JSONL * Asking price: $5,000 * Individual country datasets are also offered for sale The actor claims the dataset includes records from numerous countries, including: * India: ~1.36B * China: ~848M * United States: ~297M * Indonesia: ~245M * Nigeria: ~221M * Pakistan: ~220M * Brazil: ~182M * Bangladesh: ~151M * Ethiopia: ~118M * Mexico: ~113M * Russia: ~112M Analyst Note: The authenticity and originality of this dataset have not been independently verified. Large identity aggregation databases frequently combine information from historical breaches, public records, contact syncing services, and previously leaked datasets rather than representing a single new compromise. Regardless, such collections significantly increase the effectiveness of phishing, smishing, social engineering, and identity profiling campaigns. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Truecaller

posts.text: 🇫🇷 French Fitness Chain KeepCool Allegedly Suffers Data Exposure A threat actor claims to have exploited multiple vulnerabilities affecting French fitness chain KeepCool (http://keepcool.fr) and is offering what they describe as a database containing 287,842 member records. According to the post, the actor alleges they: * Extracted hardcoded administrator credentials from the mobile application. * Obtained a JWT token. * Exploited a BOLA (Broken Object Level Authorization) vulnerability in the API to enumerate and extract member data. The allegedly exposed data includes: * Full names * Email addresses * Phone numbers * Dates of birth * Gender * Membership plans * Club locations * Last gym visit information The actor also claims KeepCool did not notify France's CNIL after being informed of the issues. Analyst Note: These allegations have not been independently verified. If confirmed, the claimed attack chain highlights multiple application security failures, including hardcoded credentials and BOLA vulnerabilities, both of which are common issues identified in modern API security assessments. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France
posts.text_zh: 🇫🇷 法國健身連鎖店 KeepCool 據稱遭受資料外洩 威脅行為者聲稱已利用影響法國健身連鎖店 KeepCool (http://keepcool.fr) 的多個漏洞，並提供他們所描述的包含 287,842 條會員記錄的資料庫。根據該帖子，該行為者聲稱他們： * 從行動應用程式中提取了硬編碼的管理員憑證。 * 獲得JWT令牌。 * 利用API中的BOLA（破壞物件層級授權）漏洞來列舉和擷取會員資料。據稱洩露的數據包括： * 全名 * 電子郵件地址 * 電話號碼 * 出生日期 * 性別 * 會員計劃 * 俱樂部地點 * 最後一次去健身房的信息 該演員還聲稱 KeepCool 在得知這些問題後沒有通知法國 CNIL。分析師註：這些指控尚未獲得獨立證實。如果得到證實，所聲稱的攻擊鏈將突出顯示多個應用程式安全故障，包括硬編碼憑證和 BOLA 漏洞，這兩者都是現代 API 安全評估中發現的常見問題。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

posts.text_zh: 聊天，我不斷收到 DEFCON 邀請。但是，我不想和任何人說話，而且我覺得如果沒有電腦來攻擊惡意軟體，我會感到無聊。我不想和人說話。不過，我有點想看一些演講並買一些愚蠢的襯衫。你怎麼認為？

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: 🇹🇳 Webmail Access for Major Tunisian ISPs Allegedly Offered for Sale A threat actor is advertising what they claim is unauthorized webmail access associated with two major Tunisian internet service providers: * Topnet (@topnet.tn) * Orange Tunisia (@orange.tn) The seller explicitly promotes the access for phishing operations and describes the domains as highly trusted and valuable. Screenshots were shared as alleged proof of access. Analyst Note: The claims have not been independently verified. If authentic, compromised ISP-hosted mailboxes could be abused for highly convincing phishing, business email compromise, credential theft, and attacks against customers and partner organizations. The affected providers should investigate the accounts shown, revoke active sessions, reset credentials, and review authentication and mailbox-access logs. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Tunisia
posts.text_zh: 🇹🇳 據稱出售突尼斯主要 ISP 的網絡郵件訪問權限 威脅行為者正在宣傳他們聲稱與突尼斯兩大互聯網服務提供商相關的未經授權的網絡郵件訪問權限： * Topnet (@topnet.tn) * Orange Tunisia (@orange.tn) 賣家明確宣傳網絡釣魚操作的訪問權限，並將這些域名描述為高度可信且有價值。螢幕截圖被分享作為所謂的訪問證據。分析師註：這些說法尚未經過獨立核實。如果真實，受損的 ISP 託管郵箱可能會被濫用，用於極具說服力的網路釣魚、商業電子郵件洩露、憑證盜竊以及針對客戶和合作夥伴組織的攻擊。受影響的提供者應調查顯示的帳戶、撤銷活動會話、重設憑證並檢查驗證和郵箱存取日誌。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #突尼斯

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: 🤖 Alleged BloxytAI User Database Leaked on a Cybercrime Forum A threat actor has published what they claim is a user database belonging to BloxytAI, an AI-powered platform. According to the forum post: * The leaked dataset allegedly contains 103 user records. * The data is shared in CSV format. * Claimed fields include user ID, email address, subscription plan, available credits, used credits, credit limits, trial status, account start date, and downgrade status. Although the dataset appears relatively small, subscription and account metadata can provide attackers with valuable information for phishing, credential stuffing, and targeted social engineering campaigns against platform users. Analyst Note: The authenticity of the leaked data has not been independently verified. AI service providers should investigate the claim, review access logs for unauthorized activity, and ensure customer account information is adequately protected. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #AI
posts.text_zh: 🤖 據稱 BloxytAI 用戶資料庫在網路犯罪論壇上洩露 威脅行為者發布了他們聲稱屬於 AI 驅動平台 BloxytAI 的用戶資料庫。根據論壇貼文： * 洩漏的資料集據稱包含 103 筆使用者記錄。 * 資料以 CSV 格式共用。 * 已聲明欄位包含使用者 ID、電子郵件地址、訂閱方案、可用積分、已使用積分、信用額度、試用狀態、帳戶開始日期和降級狀態。儘管資料集看起來相對較小，但訂閱和帳戶元資料可以為攻擊者提供有價值的信息，用於網路釣魚、撞庫和針對平台使用者的有針對性的社會工程活動。分析師註：洩漏資料的真實性尚未得到獨立驗證。人工智慧服務提供者應調查該索賠，檢查訪問日誌是否有未經授權的活動，並確保客戶帳戶資訊得到充分保護。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #AI

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: 🇧🇷 Alleged Appmax Source Code Listed for Sale on a Cybercrime Forum A threat actor is claiming to sell the full source code of Appmax, a Brazilian payment gateway, on a cybercrime forum. According to the forum post: * The seller claims to possess more than 300 source code repositories. * The advertised archive is approximately 6.3 GB in size. * The actor states the source code will be sold and threatens to leak it publicly if the company does not respond within 10 days. * Partial repository samples and proof files were shared to support the claim. If authentic, exposure of the source code could enable attackers to identify undisclosed vulnerabilities, reverse engineer internal systems, bypass security controls, and launch targeted attacks against the platform and its customers. Analyst Note: The authenticity of the claimed source code has not been independently verified. Organizations facing source code exposure should immediately investigate the claim, rotate exposed secrets, review repositories for embedded credentials, and conduct accelerated security assessments to identify potential risks. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Brazil
posts.text_zh: 🇧🇷 涉嫌在網路犯罪論壇上出售 Appmax 原始碼 威脅行為者聲稱在網路犯罪論壇上出售巴西支付網關 Appmax 的完整原始碼。根據論壇貼文： * 賣家聲稱擁有 300 多個原始碼儲存庫。 * 公佈的存檔大小約為 6.3 GB。 * 該行為者表示，如果公司不在 10 天內回應，他們將出售原始程式碼，並威脅將其公開洩露。 * 共享部分存儲庫樣本和證明文件以支持該聲明。如果是真實的，原始程式碼的暴露可能使攻擊者能夠識別未公開的漏洞，對內部系統進行逆向工程，繞過安全控制，並對平台及其客戶發動有針對性的攻擊。分析師註釋：所聲稱的原始程式碼的真實性尚未得到獨立驗證。面臨原始程式碼暴露的組織應立即調查該聲明，輪換暴露的秘密，檢查儲存庫中的嵌入式憑證，並進行加速安全評估以識別潛在風險。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #巴西

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: 🇷🇸 Alleged http://NovostiOglasi.rs Database Listed for Sale on a Cybercrime Forum A threat actor is claiming to sell a database allegedly belonging to http://NovostiOglasi.rs, a major Serbian online classifieds platform operated by Novosti. According to the forum post: * The platform provides classified listings for real estate, vehicles, jobs, services, pets, and other marketplace categories. * The seller claims to possess the platform's database and has shared sample records. * The sample suggests the database may contain user account information, including usernames, email addresses, password hashes, registration details, and profile metadata. If authentic, the exposed data could enable credential stuffing attacks, account takeovers, phishing campaigns, and identity-related fraud targeting platform users. Analyst Note: The authenticity of the claimed database has not been independently verified. Organizations should investigate the claim, review authentication logs for suspicious activity, and ensure password hashing algorithms and credential security controls remain up to date. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Serbia
posts.text_zh: 🇷🇸 據稱 http://NovostiOglasi.rs 資料庫在網路犯罪論壇上列出出售 一名威脅行為者聲稱出售據稱屬於 http://NovostiOglasi.rs 的資料庫，該資料庫是 Novosti 經營的塞爾維亞主要線上分類平台。根據論壇貼文： * 該平台提供房地產、車輛、工作、服務、寵物和其他市場類別的分類清單。 * 賣家聲稱擁有平台資料庫並分享樣品記錄。 * 此範例顯示資料庫可能包含使用者帳戶信息，包括使用者名稱、電子郵件地址、密碼雜湊、註冊詳細資訊和個人資料元資料。如果真實，暴露的資料可能會導致撞庫攻擊、帳戶接管、網路釣魚活動以及針對平台使用者的身份相關詐欺。分析師註釋：所聲稱資料庫的真實性尚未得到獨立驗證。組織應調查該聲明，檢查身分驗證日誌是否有可疑活動，並確保密碼雜湊演算法和憑證安全控制保持最新。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Serbia

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: 🇫🇷 Alleged PPA Business School Database Listed on a Cybercrime Forum A threat actor has claimed to be selling a fresh SQL dump allegedly belonging to PPA Business School, one of France's private higher education institutions. According to the forum post: * The dataset allegedly contains approximately 293,967 records. * The database reportedly includes prospects, applicants, enrolled students, and alumni. * Claimed data fields include full names, email addresses, phone numbers, postal addresses, dates of birth, nationality, student IDs, school, class, academic year, and enrollment status. * The actor claims the dump is dated July 10, 2026. If authentic, the exposed information could facilitate phishing campaigns, identity theft, business email compromise, and targeted attacks against students, alumni, and staff. Analyst Note: The authenticity of the dataset has not been independently verified. Educational institutions should review access controls protecting student information, investigate for unauthorized database access, and monitor for signs of data misuse. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France
posts.text_zh: 🇫🇷 網路犯罪論壇上列出的涉嫌 PPA 商學院資料庫 一名威脅行為者聲稱正在出售據稱屬於法國私立高等教育機構之一 PPA 商學院的新 SQL 轉儲。根據論壇貼文： * 據稱該資料集包含約 293,967 筆記錄。 * 據報道，資料庫包括潛在客戶、申請人、在校學生和校友。 * 聲明的資料欄位包括全名、電子郵件地址、電話號碼、郵寄地址、出生日期、國籍、學生 ID、學校、班級、學年和入學狀態。 * 該攻擊者聲稱轉儲的日期為 2026 年 7 月 10 日。如果屬實，暴露的資訊可能會促進網路釣魚活動、身分盜竊、商業電子郵件洩露以及針對學生、校友和工作人員的有針對性的攻擊。分析師註：資料集的真實性尚未經過獨立驗證。教育機構應審查保護學生資訊的存取控制，調查未經授權的資料庫訪問，並監控資料濫用的跡象。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: 🪙 Alleged http://CoinTracker.io Database Listed for Sale on a Cybercrime Forum A threat actor is claiming to sell a database allegedly belonging to http://CoinTracker.io, a cryptocurrency portfolio tracking and tax reporting platform. According to the forum post: * The database allegedly contains approximately 2.7 million records. * Claimed fields include full name, date of birth, gender, email address, phone number, physical address, city, country, ZIP code, preferred language, and account status. * The seller advertises the dataset as containing worldwide users. If authentic, the exposed information could be leveraged for phishing campaigns, identity theft, cryptocurrency-targeted scams, and account takeover attempts. Analyst Note: The authenticity of the claimed database has not been independently verified. Cryptocurrency platform users should remain alert for phishing emails, verify all communications, and enable multi-factor authentication to reduce account compromise risks. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Crypto
posts.text_zh: 🪙 据称 http://CoinTracker.io 数据库在网络犯罪论坛上列出出售 威胁行为者声称出售据称属于 http://CoinTracker.io（加密货币投资组合跟踪和税务报告平台）的数据库。根據論壇貼文： * 據稱該資料庫包含約 270 萬筆記錄。 * 聲明的欄位包括全名、出生日期、性別、電子郵件地址、電話號碼、實際地址、城市、國家/地區、郵遞區號、首選語言和帳戶狀態。 * 賣方宣傳此資料集包含全球用戶。如果真實，暴露的資訊可用於網路釣魚活動、身分盜竊、針對加密貨幣的詐騙和帳戶接管嘗試。分析師註釋：所聲稱資料庫的真實性尚未得到獨立驗證。加密貨幣平台用戶應對網路釣魚電子郵件保持警惕，驗證所有通訊並啟用多重身份驗證以降低帳戶外洩風險。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Crypto

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: Pinned: 🚨 Zimbra Warns of Critical Classic Web Client Vulnerability Zimbra has disclosed a critical vulnerability affecting its Classic Web Client that could allow specially crafted emails to execute malicious JavaScript when opened. Key details: * The flaw is a stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability. * Successful exploitation could enable arbitrary code execution within a victim's web session. * Attackers may gain access to mailbox contents, session tokens, or account settings. * No CVE identifier has been assigned at the time of disclosure. * Zimbra is urging customers to apply the latest security updates immediately. Organizations still relying on the Classic Web Client should prioritize patching and consider restricting or disabling the legacy interface until updates are applied. Analyst Note: Email-based stored XSS vulnerabilities can be particularly dangerous because they require minimal user interaction and may be leveraged for session hijacking, credential theft, and lateral movement within enterprise environments. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Zimbra
posts.text_zh: 已修正：🚨 Zimbra 就嚴重經典 Web 用戶端漏洞發出警告 Zimbra 揭露了一個影響其經典 Web 用戶端的嚴重漏洞，該漏洞可能允許特製電子郵件在開啟時執行惡意 JavaScript。關鍵細節： * 此缺陷為儲存型跨站腳本 (XSS) 漏洞。 * 成功利用該漏洞可以在受害者的網路會話中執行任意程式碼。 * 攻擊者可能會獲得對郵箱內容、會話令牌或帳戶設定的存取權限。 * 揭露時尚未指派 CVE 識別碼。 * Zimbra 敦促客戶立即套用最新的安全更新。仍依賴經典 Web 用戶端的組織應優先考慮修補並考慮限製或停用舊介面，直到應用程式更新。分析師註釋：基於電子郵件的儲存型 XSS 漏洞可能特別危險，因為它們需要最少的用戶交互，並且可能被用於會話劫持、憑證盜竊和企業環境中的橫向移動。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Zimbra

posts.name: DarkWebInformer
posts.text: 🚨🇺🇸 Burger King helpdesk administrator access allegedly listed for sale A forum actor claims to be selling administrator access to one of Burger King’s helpdesk systems, providing control over customer orders, support tickets, and internal management functions. The listing claims the account can view full order and ticket details, interact with customers, create or delete tickets, change custom settings, and create, prepare, or mark orders for pickup. The actor also says the access could be used to impersonate an employee. The access is listed for $400 and described as non-negotiable. This claim is currently unverified. 💥 Get early visibility into underground claims, including unblurred screenshots, before they turn into headlines: http://darkwebinformer.com/pricing
posts.text_zh: 🚨🇺🇸 據稱出售漢堡王服務台管理員訪問權限 一名論壇參與者聲稱正在出售漢堡王幫助台系統之一的管理員訪問權限，提供對客戶訂單、支持票證和內部管理功能的控制。該列表聲稱該帳戶可以查看完整的訂單和票證詳細信息，與客戶互動，創建或刪除票證，更改自訂設定以及創建、準備或標記要取貨的訂單。該演員還表示，該存取權限可用於冒充員工。存取權限標價為 400 美元，且不可協商。目前這一說法尚未得到證實。 💥 在地下索賠成為頭條新聞之前儘早了解它們，包括清晰的螢幕截圖：http://darkwebinformer.com/pricing

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: 🇫🇷 Alleged IDOR Vulnerability Exposes Data from http://Litige.fr A threat actor has claimed to have discovered a second Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability affecting http://Litige.fr, a French legal dispute resolution platform. According to the forum post: * The actor claims to have identified a second vulnerable endpoint. * Allegedly exposed data includes SIRET numbers, company names, company types, business addresses, professions, and creditors' dates of birth. * The actor claims approximately 2.3 million user records were compromised. If confirmed, this would represent a significant exposure of business and personal information, potentially enabling identity theft, fraud, and targeted social engineering campaigns. Analyst Note: The claims have not been independently verified. Organizations operating web applications should review object-level authorization controls, test for IDOR vulnerabilities, and monitor for unauthorized access to sensitive records. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France
posts.text_zh: 🇫🇷 涉嫌 IDOR 漏洞暴露 http://Litige.fr 的資料 威脅行為者聲稱發現了第二個不安全直接物件參考 (IDOR) 漏洞，影響法國法律糾紛解決平台 http://Litige.fr。根據論壇貼文： * 攻擊者聲稱已識別出第二個易受攻擊的端點。 * 據稱暴露的資料包括 SIRET 號碼、公司名稱、公司類型、營業地址、職業和債權人的出生日期。 * 該行為者聲稱約 230 萬筆用戶記錄遭到外洩。如果得到證實，這將意味著商業和個人資訊的大量暴露，可能導致身份盜竊、詐欺和有針對性的社會工程活動。分析師註：這些說法尚未經過獨立核實。運作 Web 應用程式的組織應審查物件層級授權控制、測試 IDOR 漏洞並監控對敏感記錄的未經授權的存取。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: 🇩🇪 Alleged Database Leak Targets http://Kulturigo.de A threat actor has advertised what they claim is a WordPress database belonging to http://kulturigo.de, a Germany-based cultural platform. According to the forum post: * Alleged SQL database size: ~380 MB * Sample data appears to include WordPress user records and configuration data * The actor shared screenshots as proof of possession and is offering the database for download If authentic, exposed WordPress databases may contain user accounts, password hashes, email addresses, site configuration details, and plugin or application settings that could facilitate follow-on attacks. Analyst Note: The authenticity of the alleged database has not been independently verified. Organizations operating WordPress environments should review for unauthorized access, rotate administrative credentials, update plugins/themes, and monitor for suspicious authentication activity. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Germany
posts.text_zh: 🇩🇪 涉嫌資料庫洩漏目標 http://Kulturigo.de 威脅行為者宣傳了他們聲稱屬於德國文化平台 http://kulturigo.de 的 WordPress 資料庫。根據論壇帖子： * 據稱 SQL 資料庫大小：約 380 MB * 範例資料似乎包括 WordPress 用戶記錄和配置資料 * 攻擊者分享了螢幕截圖作為所有權證明，並提供資料庫供下載 如果真實，暴露的 WordPress 資料庫可能包含使用者帳戶、密碼雜湊、電子郵件地址、網站配置詳細資訊以及可能促進後續攻擊的插件或應用程式設定。分析师注：所称数据库的真实性尚未得到独立验证。經營 WordPress 環境的組織應檢查未經授權的存取、輪換管理憑證、更新外掛程式/主題，並監控可疑的身份驗證活動。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Germany

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: 🇲🇦 Moroccan E-Commerce Platform Allegedly Leaked A threat actor has published what they claim is the full database of http://PlanetSport.ma, a Moroccan online retailer specializing in sports apparel and equipment. According to the forum post, the actor claims to possess the platform's complete database and is distributing it through file-sharing services. While the post does not disclose the full schema, it advertises a "full leak" and provides proof-of-data alongside download links. Analyst Note: The authenticity and scope of the alleged breach have not been independently verified. If confirmed, customers should remain alert for phishing attempts, credential stuffing attacks, and unauthorized account activity, particularly if passwords were reused across multiple services. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Morocco
posts.text_zh: 🇲🇦 摩洛哥電子商務平台據稱被洩露 一名威脅行為者發布了他們聲稱的 http://PlanetSport.ma 的完整數據庫，這是一家專門從事運動服裝和裝備的摩洛哥在線零售商。根據論壇帖子，該攻擊者聲稱擁有該平台的完整資料庫，並透過文件共享服務進行分發。雖然該帖子沒有披露完整的架構，但它宣傳了“完全洩漏”，並提供了數據證明以及下載連結。分析師註：所指控的違規行為的真實性和範圍尚未得到獨立核實。如果得到證實，客戶應該對網路釣魚嘗試、撞庫攻擊和未經授權的帳戶活動保持警惕，特別是在多個服務中重複使用密碼的情況下。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Morocco

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: 🇩🇪 German Website Database Allegedly Leaked A threat actor has published what they claim is a free database belonging to http://idventure.de, a Germany-based website. According to the forum post, the leaked dataset appears to contain user account information, including: * Usernames * Email addresses * Display names * Password hashes * User activation keys * Registration timestamps * Website URLs (where applicable) * Account status information The actor is distributing the database free of charge and has provided sample records from the alleged dump. Analyst Note: The authenticity of the leak has not been independently verified. If genuine, exposed account credentials could enable credential stuffing attacks against users who reuse passwords across multiple online services. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Germany
posts.text_zh: 🇩🇪 德國網站資料庫據稱遭洩露 威脅行為者發布了他們聲稱屬於德國網站 http://idventure.de 的免費資料庫。根據論壇帖子，洩露的數據集似乎包含用戶帳戶信息，包括： * 用戶名 * 電子郵件地址 * 顯示名稱 * 密碼哈希 * 用戶激活密鑰 * 註冊時間戳 * 網站 URL（如果適用） * 帳戶狀態信息 攻擊者正在免費分發數據庫，並提供了來自所謂轉儲的樣本​​記錄。分析師註：洩漏的真實性尚未得到獨立驗證。如果是真實的，暴露的帳戶憑證可能會對在多個線上服務中重複使用密碼的使用者發動撞庫攻擊。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Germany

posts.text: 🚨🇮🇹🌍 Two enterprise administration access listings allegedly posted for sale A forum actor claims to be selling super-admin access to the IT helpdesk of an unnamed Italian multinational with approximately $2.5 billion in revenue. The listing says the account provides company-wide access to client management, messages, live logs, campaign services, groups, and database import/export functions. The actor lists the access for $300 and promotes it for data extraction and support-staff impersonation. The same actor is also advertising access to a private IRIS network operations dashboard covering 974 devices and more than 7,500 monitoring entries. The claimed support-technician account can view device locations, health data, alarms, incident causes, historical activity, and detailed system information. That access is listed for $50. These claims are currently unverified. 💥 Get early visibility into underground claims, including unblurred screenshots, before they turn into headlines: http://darkwebinformer.com/pricing

posts.text: 🚨Hostinger-hosted Colombian sites allegedly compromised A forum actor claims to have breached a Hostinger shared-hosting environment used by 61 Colombian domains, including Certifica, GuiaPix, and Colombians Lens. The actor says the data has not yet been publicly released but threatens to publish it unless the affected operators respond. posts.text: 🇷🇺 Alfa-Bank Customer Database Allegedly Offered for Sale A threat actor is advertising the alleged sale of a partial customer database belonging to Russia's Alfa-Bank. According to the forum post, the dataset allegedly contains: * Over 1.03 million customer records * Account IDs * Email addresses * Full names * Dates of birth * Payment card numbers * Primary phone numbers * Additional contact information The actor claims the data is in CSV format and references records as recent as July 2, 2026. Analyst Note: These claims have not been independently verified. If authentic, the exposed combination of personal information and financial data could significantly increase the risk of identity theft, financial fraud, and targeted phishing campaigns against affected customers. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Russia
posts.text_zh: 🇷🇺 據稱出售阿爾法銀行客戶資料庫 威脅行為者正在宣傳據稱出售屬於俄羅斯阿爾法銀行的部分客戶資料庫。根據論壇帖子，該資料集據稱包含： * 超過 103 萬筆客戶記錄 * 帳戶 ID * 電子郵件地址 * 全名 * 出生日期 * 支付卡號碼 * 主要電話號碼 * 其他聯絡資訊 攻擊者聲稱資料採用 CSV 格式，並引用了最新至 2026 年 7 月 2 日的記錄。分析師註：這些說法尚未經過獨立驗證。如果是真實的，暴露的個人資訊和財務資料組合可能會顯著增加身分盜竊、財務詐欺和針對受影響客戶的有針對性的網路釣魚活動的風險。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Russia

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: Pinned: 🚀 Google Launches Code Wiki for AI-Powered Code Understanding Google has released Code Wiki in public preview, allowing developers to generate an interactive AI-powered wiki for public code repositories. * Automatically creates and updates repository documentation. * Chat with your codebase using Gemini-powered AI. * Generates architecture, class, and sequence diagrams. * Links every explanation directly to the relevant source code. * Public repositories are supported today, with private repository support planned via Gemini CLI. Analyst Note: AI coding is rapidly evolving beyond code generation. The next frontier is code understanding—helping developers navigate unfamiliar repositories, onboard faster, audit software, and analyze large codebases with significantly less manual effort. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Google
posts.text_zh: 固定：🚀 谷歌推出用於人工智慧驅動的程式碼理解的 Code Wiki 谷歌已發佈公共預覽版的 Code Wiki，允許開發人員為公共程式碼儲存庫生成互動式人工智慧驅動的 wiki。 * 自動建立和更新儲存庫文件。 * 使用 Gemini 支援的 AI 與您的程式碼庫聊天。 * 產生架構圖、類別圖和序列圖。 * 將每個解釋直接連結到相關原始碼。 * 目前支援公共儲存庫，並計劃透過 Gemini CLI 支援私人儲存庫。分析師註釋：人工智慧編碼正在快速發展，超越了程式碼生成。下一個前沿領域是程式碼理解——幫助開發人員導航不熟悉的儲存庫、更快地上線、審核軟體並以顯著減少的手動工作分析大型程式碼庫。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Google

posts.name: DailyDarkWeb
posts.text: 🇪🇺 EU Extends Temporary "Chat Control" Rules The European Parliament has backed the return of temporary rules allowing online platforms to voluntarily scan certain user communications for child sexual abuse material (CSAM). * More MEPs voted against (314) than in favor (276), but opponents fell short of the 361 votes needed to block the measure. * The temporary rules would remain in force until 2028 unless replaced sooner. * Parliament voted to exempt end-to-end encrypted services such as WhatsApp, Signal, and Telegram. Analyst Note: The vote highlights the ongoing tension between child protection and digital privacy. While supporters view voluntary scanning as an important investigative tool, critics warn that expanding content scanning on non-encrypted platforms could set a broader precedent for online surveillance. #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #EU
posts.text_zh: 🇪🇺 歐盟延長臨時「聊天控制」規則 歐洲議會支持恢復臨時規則，允許線上平台自願掃描某些使用者通訊中是否存在兒童性虐待材料（CSAM）。 * 投票反對的歐洲議會議員 (314) 多於贊成 (276)，但反對者未達到阻止該措施所需的 361 票。 * 除非更早更換，否則臨時規則將一直有效到 2028 年。 * 議會投票豁免 WhatsApp、Signal 和 Telegram 等端對端加密服務。分析師註：此次投票凸顯了兒童保護和數位隱私之間持續存在的緊張關係。雖然支持者將自願掃描視為重要的調查工具，但批評者警告說，在非加密平台上擴大內容掃描可能會為線上監控開創更廣泛的先例。 #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #EU

posts.name: vxunderground
posts.text: Chat, I have to do a weird rant opinion thingy and I hope someone understand what I'm saying. I've noticed a strange phenomena where people who grew up poor have this weird mentality like, they feel they're not good enough to not be poor? I was "poor" growing up, not poor-poor living in a shack, but money was definitely tight and sometimes dinner sucked. Anyway, I've met TONS of people who come from lower class families and lives, and who act scared to try to pivot into cybersecurity, or computer-science in general. I don't know if it's self-esteem, or some weird internal thingie on classism, I don't know. Maybe I sound crazy. Maybe someone smarter than me can convey the idea better. But it 100% happens. I see genuinely intelligent people, definitely capable of performing the type of work I do, end up doing construction or working a dead-end job somewhere and struggling financially and hating their lives because they're not intellectually stimulated. They could do so much more ... but they don't.
posts.text_zh: 聊天，我必須做一些奇怪的咆哮意見事情，我希望有人明白我在說什麼。我注意到一個奇怪的現象，那些在貧窮中長大的人有一種奇怪的心態，例如，他們覺得自己不夠好，不能不貧窮？我從小就很“窮”，不是住在棚屋裡的窮人，但錢確實很緊張，有時晚餐很糟糕。不管怎樣，我見過很多來自下層家庭和生活的人，他們表現得害怕嘗試轉向網路安全或一般的電腦科學。我不知道這是自尊，還是階級主義的一些奇怪的內在因素，我不知道。也許我聽起來很瘋狂。也許比我聰明的人更能傳達這個想法。但這是100%發生的。我看到真正聰明的人，絕對有能力從事我所做的工作類型，最終在某個地方從事建築工作或從事沒有出路的工作，在經濟上陷入困境，並討厭他們的生活，因為他們沒有智力上的刺激。他們可以做得更多……但他們沒有。

posts.text: ⚠️ 11 Million US Resumes and PII Allegedly Leaked on Darkweb Forum A darkweb forum post claims the exposure of a massive dataset containing 11 million detailed resumes from US job seekers, totaling 1TB of data. The actor alleges that the collection spans over 20 years and includes sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) such as email addresses, phone numbers, and full home addresses. While the specific source of the breach is not identified in the listing, the actor offers a sample preview to demonstrate data quality before directing interested parties to contact them via Telegram. According to the post, the dataset includes a metadata file (meta.csv) with the following fields: - sjt_user_id: Unique seeker ID - register_date: Account registration date - firstname: First name - lastname: Last name - email: Primary email address - phone: Primary phone number - address: Street address - city: City - state: State/province code - zip: ZIP/postal code - country: Country code - dockey: Resume document key (MD5) - original_name: Original uploaded resume filename - resume_size: File size in bytes - create_date: Resume creation timestamp - modified_date: Last resume update timestamp - saved_filename: Decoded resume filename inside files/ (PDF/DOCX/DOC) The sample preview provided in the thread lists three records with names, emails, phone numbers, cities, states, ZIP codes, and associated resume filenames in DOCX format. If authentic, this leak could facilitate large-scale identity theft, targeted phishing campaigns, and social engineering attacks against individuals across the United States. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, nor has the original source of the data been confirmed. #DataBreach #PII #IdentityTheft #DarkWeb #USPrivacy #ThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity
posts.text_zh: ⚠️ 1100 萬份美國簡歷和 PII 據稱在暗網論壇上洩露 一篇暗網論壇帖子聲稱，包含 1100 萬份美國求職者詳細簡歷、總計 1TB 數據的海量數據集被曝光。該演員聲稱該集合跨越 20 多年，其中包括敏感的個人識別資訊 (PII)，例如電子郵件地址、電話號碼和完整家庭住址。雖然清單中沒有指出違規的具體來源，但攻擊者提供了一個範例預覽來展示資料質量，然後指示感興趣的各方透過 Telegram 聯繫他們。根據該帖子，該資料集包括一個元資料檔案 (meta.csv)，其中包含以下欄位： - sjt_user_id：唯一搜尋者 ID - register_date：帳戶註冊日期 - 名字：名字 - 姓氏：姓氏 - 電子郵件：主要電子郵件地址 - 電話：主要電話號碼 - 電話號碼 - 城市簡歷：國家：7 城市代碼 - 城市代碼：國家/城市(MD5) -原始名稱：原始上傳的簡歷文件名 - 簡歷大小：文件大小（以字節為單位） - 創建日期：簡歷創建時間戳 - 修改日期：上次簡歷更新時間戳 - 保存文件名：文件/（PDF/DOCX/DOC）內解碼的簡歷文件名 線程中提供的示例預覽格式列表了文件記錄，其中 DOC如果屬實，這次洩漏可能會助長針對美國各地個人的大規模身分盜竊、有針對性的網路釣魚活動和社會工程攻擊。截至報告時，所指控的違規行為尚未得到獨立核實，數據的原始來源也尚未得到確認。 #DataBreach #PII #IdentityTheft #DarkWeb #USPrivacy #ThreatIntelligence #Cyber​​Security

posts.text: 🚨 Threat Actor Claims Massive FortiGate Credential Leak Affecting Spotify, Samsung, and Oracle A darkweb forum post reveals the alleged sale of a large dataset containing credentials tied to FortiGate SSL-VPN portals and administrative access points. The actor claims the dataset, labeled as "FortiBleed dataset 2026," contains 68,520 total credential records spanning 21,387 distinct domains. According to the listing, the data includes masked login URLs, corporate email addresses (FortiGuardID), and the country of access for each record. The post alleges that credentials for several major global technology and enterprise organizations are included in the leak. The top affected domains by estimated user count listed in the sample include: * http://spotify.com – Music Streaming – 4,223,908 users * http://samsung.com – Consumer Electronics – 2,325,467 users * http://spe.sony.com – Entertainment Media – 1,958,292 users * http://mihoyo.com – Video Games – 723,402 users * http://oracle.com – Enterprise Software – 704,733 users * http://mercadolivre.com – E-commerce Marketplace – 694,280 users * http://service.alibaba.com – E-commerce Marketplace – 611,722 users * http://huawei.com – Telecommunications Equipment – 478,509 users * http://epfindia.gov.in – Government Services – 339,113 users * http://lenovo.com – Computer Hardware – 272,000 users The seller claims that 400 to 500 of the accessed credentials remain valid. The geographic distribution of the records reportedly shows significant activity from India (9,371 records), the USA (6,151 records), Taiwan (3,419 records), Mexico (3,149 records), and Turkey (2,894 records). The file size is listed as 8.68 MB. If authentic, exposure of valid FortiGate VPN credentials could enable unauthorized remote access to internal corporate networks, potentially leading to lateral movement and data exfiltration within these high-value targets. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by the affected organizations or security researchers.
posts.text_zh: 🚨 威脅行為者聲稱大規模 FortiGate 憑證洩露影響了 Spotify、三星和 Oracle 一篇暗網論壇帖子揭露了涉嫌出售包含與 FortiGate SSL-VPN 門戶和管理接入點相關憑證的大型資料集的行為。該攻擊者聲稱該資料集標記為“FortiBleed dataset 2026”，包含跨越 21,387 個不同域的 68,520 條憑證記錄。根據清單，資料包括封鎖的登入 URL、公司電子郵件地址 (FortiGuardID) 以及每筆記錄的造訪國家/地區。該貼文稱，洩漏的資訊包含了全球幾家主要技術和企業組織的憑證。樣本中列出的估計用戶數受影響最大的網域包括： * http://spotify.com – 音樂串流媒體 – 4,223,908 位用戶 * http://samsung.com – 消費性電子 – 2,325,467 位用戶 * http://spe.sony.com – 娛樂媒體 – 1,958,292 位元用戶 * http://spe.sony.com – 娛樂媒體 – 1,958,292 位元遊戲http://oracle.com – 企業軟體 – 704,733 位使用者 * http://mercadolivre.com – 電子商務市場 – 694,280 位使用者 * http://service.alibaba.com – 電子商務市場 – 611,722 位使用者 * http://huawei.com – 電信設備 – 電子商務市場 – 611,722 位使用者 * http://huawei.com – 電信設備 – 478,50935625002 政府服務.位元使用者 * http://lenovo.com – 電腦硬體– 272,000 個使用者 賣家聲稱 400 到 500 個存取的憑證仍然有效。根據報告，記錄的地理分佈顯示印度（9,371 筆記錄）、美國（6,151 筆記錄）、台灣（3,419 筆記錄）、墨西哥（3,149 筆記錄）和土耳其（2,894 筆記錄）有大量活動。文件大小列為 8.68 MB。如果是真實的，有效的 FortiGate VPN 憑證的暴露可能會導致對內部公司網路的未經授權的遠端訪問，從而可能導致這些高價值目標內的橫向移動和資料外洩。截至報告之時，受影響的組織或安全研究人員尚未對所指控的違規行為進行獨立核實。

posts.name: MonThreat
posts.text: 🚨 Threat Actor Claims Breach of Keccel Billing Database Exposing 3.4M Congolese Numbers A darkweb forum post reveals that a threat actor has allegedly obtained full access to the billing database of Keccel Technologies S.A.R.L, a fintech and SMS gateway provider based in Kinshasa, Democratic Republic of Congo. The actor claims to have compromised the company's entire billing and event infrastructure, exposing data related to 29 corporate clients who route A2P SMS and WhatsApp messages through major Congolese operators. According to the listing, the breach exposes significant volumes of communication metadata and subscriber information: * **3,424,158** unique phone numbers from the DRC (+243) * **35,698,321** total SMS events * **26,428,316** billable SMS messages The post details the distribution of this traffic across mobile network operators, with Airtel accounting for 39.4% of phones (1,348,955) and Vodacom holding 26.0% (890,293). Orange and Africell follow with 24.0% and 8.7% respectively. The actor also lists top sender brands utilizing the gateway, including gambling platforms like Winner Bet and betPawa, financial services such as Visa Pay, and technology firms like Altech and Africa's Talking. Sample data provided in the thread includes CSV exports containing customer IDs, organization names, creation timestamps, and billing identifiers for entities such as KECCEL, NETMOBILE, DIGIT MARKET, and MTARGET FRANCE. Additionally, the post displays logs linking specific phone numbers to sender brands and message counts, potentially revealing user engagement patterns for services ranging from banking alerts to betting notifications. Keccel Technologies operates as a critical infrastructure provider for digital communications in the region, facilitating transactions for banks, utilities, and government bodies. If authentic, the exposure of this billing data could enable targeted phishing campaigns against corporate clients or facilitate fraud by revealing active user bases for high-value services. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified.
posts.text_zh: 🚨 威胁行为者声称 Keccel 计费数据库遭到破坏，暴露了 340 万个刚果号码 暗网论坛帖子显示，威胁行为者据称已获得对 Keccel Technologies S.A.R.L（位于刚果民主共和国金沙萨的金融科技和 SMS 网关提供商）计费数据库的完全访问权限。该攻击者声称已经破坏了该公司的整个计费和活动基础设施，暴露了与 29 家企业客户相关的数据，这些客户通过刚果主要运营商路由 A2P SMS 和 WhatsApp 消息。根据清单，此次泄露暴露了大量的通信元数据和用户信息： * **3,424,158** 来自刚果民主共和国的独特电话号码 (+243) * **35,698,321** 短信事件总数 * **26,428,316** 计费短信 该帖子详细介绍了这些流量在移动网络运营商之间的分布情况，其中 Airtel 占手机数量的 39.4% (1,348,955)，而 Vodacom 則佔手機總數的 39.4%持有 26.0% (890,293)。 Orange 和 Africell 緊隨其後，分別佔 24.0% 和 8.7%。这位演员还列出了使用该网关的顶级发件人品牌，包括 Winner Bet 和 betPawa 等赌博平台、Visa Pay 等金融服务以及 Altech 和 Africa's Talking 等科技公司。该线程中提供的示例数据包括 CSV 导出，其中包含 KECCEL、NETMOBILE、DIGIT MARKET 和 MTARGET FRANCE 等实体的客户 ID、组织名称、创建时间戳和账单标识符。此外，该帖子还显示将特定电话号码与发件人品牌和消息计数相关联的日志，可能会揭示从银行警报到投注通知等服务的用户参与模式。 Keccel Technologies 是該地區數位通訊的關鍵基礎設施提供商，為銀行、公用事業和政府機構的交易提供便利。如果真实，这些计费数据的暴露可能会导致针对企业客户的有针对性的网络钓鱼活动，或者通过暴露高价值服务的活跃用户群来促进欺诈。截至本報告發布之時，所指控的違規行為尚未得到獨立核實。

It has slowly over the years mutated by people to be; "stinky", "smelly smellington", "crazy cat guy", "schnelli", "schmelly", etc. It is still mostly "smelly". It is not a cool or badass alias. She would walk into my office when I was working and, because room was small, poorly ventilated, and the door was closed, the room would get incredibly hot. I would get stinky from body odor. She would come in, kiss me on the cheek, call me her "smelly man", and give me a snack or something. I would then resume work. She has always been extremely supportive of my malware collecting, writing, reversing, etc. Hence, when I made vx-underground in 2019, and decided to start the journey of malware collecting and stuff, I said, "I need a cool name that is tough and everyone will think I'm totally awesome and badass". Then I remembered my wife called me "smelly"... so I went with that.

posts.text: In regards to these files inside of "child porn folder", the files present inside all end in *.lnk (A Windows shortcut). This is a very common file masquerading technique to give the facade of a legitimate file, but it's actually malware (shortcut pulls a file that it detonates, or points to an actual malware payload). However, there is a small chance this is not file masquerading and a legitimate child predator accidentally uploaded files which are flagged as malware due to them linking to an external domain... or something else. Basically, there are a few things this could be 1. Legitimate CSAM (Child sexual abuse material) 2. Malware payload disguised as CSAM to target pedophiles 3. Malware naming itself something CSAM related to avoid analysis Regardless, for legal reasons (and emotional), I won't even try to figure out.

posts.text: 🚨 11.4M Mexico City Citizen Records Allegedly Leaked on Darkweb Forum A darkweb forum post claims the exposure of a database containing personal information for over 11 million residents of Mexico City. The listing, posted by an actor using the handle 'moonsh1n3', alleges that the dataset includes sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) such as full names, voter IDs, dates of birth, and complete residential addresses. According to the post, the database contains 11,482,760 records in TXT format. The exposed data fields reportedly include: * Voter ID (Clave Elector) * Full Name * Date of Birth * Gender * Occupation * Full Residential Address (Street, Number, Colony, Postal Code) * INE Registration Date The samples provided in the thread show detailed address information, including street names, internal/external numbers, and specific colonies within Mexico City. The actor claims this data covers individuals residing in the capital region. Mexico City is one of the most populous metropolitan areas in the world, with a population exceeding 9 million in the city proper and over 22 million in the greater metropolitan area. A breach of this scale would represent a significant exposure of citizen data, potentially facilitating identity theft, phishing campaigns, and physical security risks for affected individuals. The source of the leak was not specified in the post, nor was any ransom demand or attribution to a specific threat group mentioned. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by Mexican authorities or cybersecurity firms.

posts.text: ⚠️ Threat Actor Claims 430M Records Leaked from Argentine Credit Bureau InfoExperto A darkweb forum post alleges the exposure of over 430 million records from InfoExperto, a major Argentine credit bureau and data broker. The actor claims to have dumped partial source code, VPN keys, SSH root keys, and production environment configurations containing hardcoded credentials from the company's servers. According to the listing, the breach includes sensitive government-issued data and financial records, including: * **Payroll/Employment Records:** Over 100 million links between employees, employers, and salaries (AFIP Form 931) through April 2026. * **National Identity Registry:** 65 million records from the AFIP padron, including full names, DNI, DOB, addresses, and status. * **Telecom Data:** Approximately 176 million cell phone records across Personal/Telecom, Claro, and Movistar, linking names, DNIs, and addresses to phone lines. * **State-Declared Contacts:** 86 million phone records and 30 million email records sourced from ANSES, IFE, and other government programs. * **Financial Data:** 245,000 Banco Columbia credit cardholders with full 16-digit PANs, plus 4,675 platform-stored cards with CVVs and expiry dates in plaintext. * **Biometric Data:** 48,000 KYC identity verifications with face match scores and raw ID card chip data. The post further claims the leak includes AFIP digital certificates that could allow impersonation of the company to query national identity registries directly. It also lists internal scrapers used to pull live data from entities such as AFIP, ANSES, BCRA, and Movistar. InfoExperto is a significant player in Argentina’s financial infrastructure, providing credit scoring and identity verification services to banks, fintechs, and law firms. The alleged exposure of plaintext CVVs and biometric data poses severe risks for identity theft and financial fraud if the data is authentic. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by InfoExperto or security researchers.

posts.text: 🚨 BONK DAO Loses Approximately $20 Million in Treasury Funds Due to Malicious Vote A malicious DAO governance proposal targeted BonkDAO, which is part of the BONK community in the Solana ecosystem. by a malicious DAO governance proposal. BONK’s official X account announced that, as a result of the incident, approximately $20 million worth of BONK tokens were drained from the BonkDAO treasury, and the exchange wallets used to purchase BONK prior to the attack have been identified. It was noted that BonkDAO is working with exchanges, bridge services, and the Solana Foundation and that law enforcement has been notified. A DAO is a governance model in which a project’s decisions A DAO operates as a governance model where token holders vote on project decisions instead of a central team, through token holders’ votes rather than a central team's. Realms is one of the platforms within the Solana ecosystem where such DAO votes take place. At the center of the incident was a proposal titled “BIP #76 - Sowellian BonkDAO” created on Realms. The proposal included statements such as “implementation of Sowellian governance, the establishment of new members and a council, rebuilding the community, evaluation of assets, and that those who vote ‘YES’ will be eligible to receive tokens.” However, a critical part of the proposal instructed that 4,426,104,450,305 BONK from the BonkDAO treasury be sent to the address 9bxWkNf3BtJ6iehq9KbX9uCWMjem4TFiPZ19T2sYJHvQ. While the vote required a 1% quorum, the 882.38B BONK “Yes” votes narrowly surpassed the 879.95B BONK quorum threshold. As a result, the proposal was approved with a 99.9% “Yes” vote. This incident is not considered a classic smart contract hack, but rather a governance attack. A governance attack occurs when an attacker gains voting power and manipulates the DAO’s decision-making mechanism to their own advantage instead of technically breaching the system. In this case, it appears that the attacker acquired enough voting power to influence the vote by purchasing BONK prior to the proposal and then passed the proposal containing the malicious transfer instruction. For this reason, DAO users must carefully review not only the proposal title or description but also the instruction details, the amount of tokens to be sent, the destination address, and any interactions with the treasury. On the DAO side, additional security measures such as high quorum requirements, timelocks, multisig approval, and emergency veto mechanisms are critical for large treasury transfers. #GovernanceAttack #Web3Security #ThreatMon #TokenGovernance #DigitalAssetSecurity

posts.text: 🚨 Threat Actor Claims Breach of Brazilian FX Broker VIPS A threat actor operating under the alias 'Kaido' claims to have breached VIPS Corretora de Cambio SA, a Brazilian foreign exchange brokerage regulated by the Central Bank of Brazil (BACEN). According to a post on an underground forum, the actor alleges they possess complete backend access and sensitive records from the company, which operates under the domains http://vipscambio.com.br and http://vipscc.com.br. The post enumerates a significant volume of compromised data and assets, allegedly including: * 115,109 unique CPFs (Brazilian taxpayer IDs) * 2,414 client bank accounts with full details * 72 internal system credentials * 3,595 signed contracts (DocuSign) * 18 corporate bank accounts * Digital certificates with passwords * Remote access to production servers * Government reporting credentials Beyond personal data, the actor alleges exposure of critical financial intelligence, claiming evidence of R$ 330 million in unreported Anti-Money Laundering (AML) operations. The post also cites financial movement logs showing R$ 262 million in transactions over a 60-day period, alongside monthly FX volumes of R$ 130 million. Additionally, the actor claims to have exposed full PII for five directors and system access details for 72 employees. VIPS Corretora de Cambio SA is a licensed financial institution in Brazil, making it subject to strict regulatory oversight by BACEN. If authentic, the alleged exposure of unreported AML operations and client financial data could result in severe regulatory penalties and reputational damage for the firm. The actor provided sample files via http://privatebin.net links to substantiate the claims. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by VIPS or regulatory authorities.

posts.text: 🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of Microsoft 365 Pre-Auth 0-Day on Darkweb A threat actor is allegedly auctioning a zero-day vulnerability targeting Microsoft 365 core infrastructure on an underground forum. The post claims the exploit allows for pre-authentication, zero-click initial access to global routing systems, bypassing perimeter security measures such as Microsoft Edge WAF and Cloudflare rules. According to the listing, the actor alleges the ability to forge an internal network bridge via a "protocol-level desynchronization vector." The post includes technical artifacts purportedly demonstrating the breach: * Internal host access: *.PROD.OUTLOOK.COM * Specific server target: http://TP3P295MB0169.TWNP295.PROD.OUTLOOK.COM * Generated session cookie: ClientId=9C1D820516E44EDDBED29972C18E3E46 * Response headers: X-ResponseOrigin: OwaNetCore The seller claims the vulnerability affects global multi-region infrastructure, including North America (nam), Europe (eur), Japan (jpn), and specific internal domains like loki.delve. The actor asserts the capability to hijack live remote HTTP data streams and perform mass account takeovers without user interaction. However, the post notes that cache poisoning is non-functional due to strict backend headers. The listing sets a starting bid of $1,000,000 USD and a "Buy It Now" price of $2,500,000 USD, payable in Monero (XMR). The actor offers live verification via forum administrators using clean tenants provided by buyers. Microsoft has not commented on this specific claim. At the time of reporting, the alleged vulnerability and its impact have not been independently verified by security researchers or Microsoft. #Microsoft #ZeroDay #DarkWeb #CyberSecurity #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence

posts.text: ⚠️ Threat Actor Claims Massive Data Leak of Grupo ATC and Major Auto Partners A threat actor has allegedly leaked over 340 GB of data from Grupo ATC, a Mexican logistics and transportation conglomerate, along with sensitive information pertaining to dozens of major global corporations. According to a post on an underground forum, the actor claims to have obtained 23 databases containing more than 2 billion unique rows after negotiations for a ransom failed. The actor alleges that because the company did not pay, they are now releasing the data to the public. The leak reportedly includes highly sensitive credentials and personally identifiable information (PII) related to Grupo ATC’s internal operations and its extensive network of partners. The actor claims the following categories of data are exposed: * OAuth2 bearer/refresh tokens and JWT tokens * Cleartext application passwords and SFTP credentials * API credentials and third-party API keys * Password hashes (bcrypt $2a$11$, PBKDF2, custom salted) * Email reset tokens and intercepted business emails * Employee PII including names, emails, phone numbers, RFCs, and bank account numbers (CLABES) * GPS position data and operational freight routes * Vehicle identification numbers with routing information * Internal source code paths and cloud storage account names The actor asserts that the breach extends beyond Grupo ATC, exposing data linked to major automotive and manufacturing giants such as Ford, Toyota, Tesla, Honda, Nissan, Mazda, Hyundai, BMW, General Motors, Stellantis, Penske, John Deere, Nestle, Mabe, and L'Oréal. The post suggests this data could be used for spear-phishing, fraud, or gaining initial access for further ransomware campaigns against these high-profile entities. Grupo ATC is a significant player in the North American logistics sector, specializing in automotive logistics and supply chain solutions. If authentic, the exposure of internal API endpoints, freight billing amounts, and employee financial details could facilitate targeted attacks on both the logistics provider and its corporate clients. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified.

posts.name: MonThreat
posts.text: 🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of 4M ISSSTE Records on Darkweb A listing on an underground forum alleges the sale of a historical database containing over 4 million records from Mexico’s Institute of Social Security and Social Services for State Workers (ISSSTE). The post, dated July 1, 2026, claims to offer access to sensitive personal and employment data belonging to state employees. According to the thread, the dataset includes a wide range of personally identifiable information (PII) and financial details. The actor lists the following data fields as being present in the dump: * CURP (Unique Population Registry Code) * RFC (Federal Taxpayer Identification Code) * NSS (Social Security Number) * ISSSTE Number * Full names (first name, father's last name, mother's last name) * Salary details (ISSSTE salary, SAR salary, total compensation) * Employment history (start date, appointment type, enrollment date) * Administrative identifiers (branch number, payroll office number, payment code) ISSSTE is a critical government entity in Mexico, providing social security benefits to millions of federal public servants. The exposure of such comprehensive employment and tax data could facilitate identity theft, social engineering attacks, and targeted fraud against state workers. The post indicates that the price for the database is negotiable and directs interested buyers to contact the seller via Signal. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by security researchers or official statements from ISSSTE.

posts.text: If you have Citrix Netscaler and logs in a SIEM somewhere, check for requests to /adfs/service/trust/2005/usernamemixed The specification is supposed to be /services/ (plural) but Citrix typo it as /service/ - our friends in China know, they use it to reach SAML on Netscaler without anybody knowing (it's a google dork... until now).
